senses.definition: to quarrel or argue with someone
having a very bad relationship with someone and be able to make further interactions with them

examples.x: They broke up on bad terms, with neither willing to forgive the other.
He is on bad terms with his former business partner after their falling out.
The two families are on bad terms due to a long-standing feud.
I'm glad that we're not on bad terms anymore, because I really miss her.

word_list.pronunciation: nˈɜːɹɪʃ ɔːɹ nˈɜːs ɔːɹ fˈɑːstɚɹ ɐ vˈaɪpɚ ɔːɹ snˈeɪk ɪn wˈʌnz bˈʊsəm

senses.definition: to support or take care of someone who turns out to be untrustworthy or harmful, despite one's good intentions

examples.x: David thought Emily was a good tenant, but he was nursing a viper in his bosom because she was damaging the property and not paying rent.
David thought Susan was a loyal employee, but he fostered a snake in his bosom because she had been stealing from the company.
I'm afraid that I've been nourishing a viper in my bosom.
John thought Mary was a trustworthy supporter, but he was nursing a snake in his bosom because she was secretly working for his political rival.
Mike realized he had fostered a viper in his bosom when Emily's behavior caused several other players to become unhappy and ultimately led to her being kicked off the team.
Tom thought he could trust his new roommate, but he soon realized he had nourished a snake in his bosom when he discovered his missing belongings.

word_list.pronunciation: nˈoʊ ɔːɹ lˈɪɾəl lˈʌv lˈɔst bɪtwˈiːn

senses.definition: used to say that two people, teams, organizations, etc. dislike one another

examples.x: In the competitive world of business, there is often little love lost between rival companies.
Sarah and her ex-husband had a bitter divorce, and there is no love lost between them.

senses.definition: a person who is only a friend during good times and is not supportive or reliable during difficult or challenging times
an attitude or behavior characterized by a lack of warmth, friendliness, or interest

examples.x: After our argument, my sister has been giving me the cold shoulder, refusing to speak to me.
Despite being in the same office, John always gives his co-workers the cold shoulder and rarely engages in conversations with them.
Despite her attempts to reconcile, he offered her a cold shoulder, refusing to engage in conversation.
She approached him with a friendly smile, but she got the cold shoulder, as he turned away without a word.
The manager displayed a cold shoulder towards the employee who had made a mistake.

senses.definition: to do something or act in a way that makes it impossible to return to previous situation or state

examples.x: Sally decided to quit her job and publicly criticize her former employer, effectively burning her bridges with the company.
The politician burned his boats by refusing to compromise on his position.

senses.definition: a life that is full of fights and arguments

examples.x: The couple's marriage turned into a cat-and-dog life.
The neighbors next door have a cat-and-dog life.
The siblings' relationship was a cat-and-dog life.

word_list.pronunciation: dɹˈɑːp ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ lˈaɪk ɐ bˈæd hˈæbɪt

senses.definition: to quickly and completely end a relationship or association with someone or something, usually due to negative or undesirable qualities or behaviors

examples.x: After realizing that the project was going nowhere, the team decided to drop it like a bad habit and focus on more promising opportunities.
Despite their long friendship, Sarah had to drop Karen like a bad habit when she discovered her manipulative and toxic behavior.

word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt ɡˈɪv ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ ˌɛni ɔːɹ hˈaʊs ɹˈuːm

senses.definition: to not tolerate or accept someone or something in a particular place or situation

examples.x: The company had strict policies against unethical practices and made it clear that they would not give corruption any house room within their organization.
